name: Bangkok

palette.name: Temple Gold
palette.rationale: Gold is a dominant visual cue in temple ornament, palace details, and ceremonial architecture.

palette.name: Chao Phraya Blue
palette.rationale: River blue anchors Bangkok's waterways, transit, and historic settlement pattern.

palette.name: Roof Tile Red
palette.rationale: Red-orange roof tiles add a recognizable temple and old-city architectural accent.

palette.name: Tropical Green
palette.rationale: A saturated green represents tropical planting, canalside vegetation, and park shade.

palette.name: Palace Cream
palette.rationale: Warm cream balances ornate gold and red with palace walls and sunlit stucco.

palette.name: Royal Purple
palette.rationale: Purple is used editorially as a ceremonial accent and should not be read as an official city color.

palette.name: Skytrain Graphite
palette.rationale: A dark transit neutral supports elevated rail, roads, and contemporary commercial districts.

palette.name: Market Pink
palette.rationale: Bright market color gives designers a small high-energy accent for food, flowers, and night streets.

sources.title: Visit Bangkok
sources.note: Used for official city tourism context.

sources.title: Tourism Authority of Thailand
sources.note: Used for national tourism and landmark context.

notes: Complete starter record; all colors are editorial readings of architecture, landscape, transit, and culture, with no official municipal color claim.
editorialSummary: Bangkok's complete palette mixes temple gold, river blue, roof red, tropical green, palace cream, ceremonial purple, transit graphite, and market pink.
